settings.about.updateDesktopOnly: Update checks are only available in the desktop app.
settings.about.note.curiousMissing: Why does it look like some features are still missing?
settings.about.note.curiousRough: Why are some interactions still rough?
settings.about.note.originStory: Almost 8 months passed from starting vlaina to the first release. At first I wanted to build a todo + calendar + notes + AI chat aggregation app. After two months I regretted it: there were too many details for one person to finish, so I deleted those two months of code and started again.
settings.about.note.migrationStory: During that time I also migrated frameworks, from Tauri to Electron. I originally chose Tauri largely because I came from C development and care a lot about size and resource usage. I once made {catimeSite} only {catimeSize} (if you do not know {catimeSite}, I strongly recommend trying it: a pure-C desktop timer with {catimeStars} stars and very low resource usage). I was confident vlaina could stay under {targetSize}, but finally decided to switch. The main reason was Linux details on my Arch setup. Tauri is great; it just was not the right fit for vlaina.
settings.about.note.releaseHeading: Why release now instead of polishing further?
settings.about.note.releaseReason: The reason is simple: these 8 months had no income, and my savings only last through this month. There are no investors, so if I do not launch now, it becomes hard to keep developing full time.
settings.about.note.donationHeading: Since this is open source, why not rely only on donations?
settings.about.note.donationReason: Because donations alone cannot support a full-time job.
settings.about.note.catimeStory: Before vlaina, I spent more than a year in university building {catimeSite}, a countdown tool I truly loved, written line by line in pure C over countless days and nights. It gained {catimeStars} stars on GitHub and more than 100,000 users. That is a huge honor. {catimeStars} may not sound intuitive, but {catimeSite} ranks second in GitHub Topics for {clockTopic}; the first is a Mac tool. All of that took about a year. The only reason there is no Mac version yet is that I could not afford a Mac, but I will support it later.
settings.about.note.earnHeading: So did such a strong project make a lot of money? Not really...
settings.about.note.catimeIncome: During that year and more, all economic return I received through {catimeSite} was about {catimeIncome}. Half came from donations by more than 50 lovely users, and the other half from one friend-referred sponsorship plus server sponsorship. For me as a student, it felt like a huge sum, haha.
settings.about.note.graduated: But now I have graduated, so I have to worry about supporting myself.
settings.about.note.jobHeading: Why not get a job and build open source on the side?
settings.about.note.jobStory: I tried, but I simply could not do it. Around 8 months ago, I joined a startup in Beijing. The hours were 10 AM to 7 PM with weekends off. At first I calculated that I should still have plenty of time for open source, but if you look at {catimeSite} commit history, there was a period with very few commits. Sometimes I did not even want to open the project folder. It was my first job in Beijing, and I had almost no savings as a student, so I lived at the last stop of Line 4 in a tiny shared room. The commute alone was almost 1.5 hours each way, roughly 3 hours per day. To save money I could not order takeout, so I cooked. From buying groceries to eating, it often became 10 PM, then I still had to shower and wake at 7 AM the next morning to make breakfast.
settings.about.note.afterWorkStory: After counting it up, there was basically no time that belonged to me. I remember coming home from work and not knowing why my old passion was gone. When I built {catimeSite}, I could work until 3 or 4 AM; at that time I did not even want to turn on the computer. I carried it home from the company every day, but usually only wanted to lie in bed and watch videos on Bilibili.
settings.about.note.weekendHeading: What about weekends? Surely there was time then?
settings.about.note.weekendStory: For some reason, I still did not want to open my computer. The rented room had a very low desk and a backless stool, and the desk could not even fit my keyboard. I also felt tired. It was very different from now, when I can work on vlaina for more than 10 hours a day and still feel energetic.
settings.about.note.resignationStory: Some work details are not convenient to share here. Later I realized I am better suited to working independently. I still wanted to build my own project, or at least something I could truly control, so I resigned. To be honest, I did not know what I would do after resigning.
settings.about.note.resignationQuoteHead: This is the message I sent my boss when I left:
settings.about.note.resignationQuote:
The future is truly strange. 8 months ago I was a nobody: nothing, no network, no {catimeSite}, really nothing. I never dreamed there would be today.
I think we will meet at the summit.
settings.about.note.newProject: And today, 8 months later, I came with my new project. I do not expect it to make a huge amount of money; I only hope it can support me.
settings.about.note.paidAnswer: Yes and no. vlaina does not force payment, and even AI providers can be fully customized. You can use every feature completely without spending money.
settings.about.note.valueAnswer: Honestly, I also do not want to make that kind of software, because I enjoy the feeling that my software can help many people. Maybe that is one way personal value is realized.
settings.about.note.supportMembership: If you want to support vlaina development and get a smoother out-of-the-box experience, please consider subscribing to our membership. This is very important to me. You can also connect your own API at zero cost.
settings.about.note.feedback: If you run into interaction or experience problems, or have good ideas, please contact the developer. I will try to release a small version every week to improve the experience. We also need lots of testing, so you are very welcome to join our community.
settings.about.note.thanks: Finally, special thanks to the friends who have supported me since {catimeSite} ヾ(๑╹ヮ╹๑)ﾉ. Truly, without you I would not have had the courage to resign, pursue what I wanted to do, or build vlaina today.
